authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Long Canyon Creek not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Long Canyon Creek not listed)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Middle Fork American River Project (FERC No. 2079) — California State Water Resources Control Board
R28 North Fork Long Canyon Creek below Diversion — Middle Fork Project flow gauges (PCWA)

USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Landforms layer records for "Long Canyon" (gaz_id 227551), "North Fork Long Canyon" (gaz_id 264182), and "South Fork Long Canyon" (gaz_id 267472), Valley class, Placer Co., CA (GNIS has no separate Stream-class record for "Long Canyon Creek" itself)
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":264182,"gaz_name":"North Fork Long Canyon","gaz_featureclass":"Valley"} and {"gaz_id":267472,"gaz_name":"South Fork Long Canyon","gaz_featureclass":"Valley"} and {"gaz_id":227551,"gaz_name":"Long Canyon","gaz_featureclass":"Valley"} all share the same Web Mercator vertex (-13415712.37, 4724715.92) as an endpoint, converting to lon -120.5154, lat 39.0212 — the confluence where the North and South Forks of Long Canyon join to form Long Canyon Creek (upstream of the North/South Fork diversion dams at T14N R13E Sec.24 MDM per the CEQA Notice of Determination for the diversion-modification projects, https://ceqanet.lci.ca.gov/2012082046/20). Corrected from the file's prior estimate (-120.48, 39.03), which was ~3.2km away and not traceable to any GNIS record; also confirmed the Stream-layer 'Long Canyon Creek' entries under gaz_id 266939 (Mariposa Co.), 272182 (Tulare Co.), and 1733847 (El Dorado Co., Slate Mountain quad, ~30km away) are unrelated same-named creeks in other counties, not this Placer County creek.

end.verbatim: Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Long Canyon Creek is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide stream regulation in §5.85(a)(2).

regulations.humanSummary: Open last Saturday in April through November 15 (general statewide stream regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water); November 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April is zero-bag, artificial-lure-with-barbless-hook only
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams, rivers, and canals during open season: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit. During the closed season (Nov 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April) the bag limit is zero and trout must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit during open season; zero-bag catch-and-release during the winter closed season
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.note: During the winter closed season (November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April), only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish, and any trout caught must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Winter closed season: artificial lures with barbless hooks only; trout must be released unharmed
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
